claim: Expert-rubric scoring showed stronger aggregate judge consistency than generic-rubric scoring.
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: Supporting: justifies testing the bundled scoring design under controls; it does not identify which component matters.

claim: Generic-rubric scoring provided a lower aggregate-agreement comparator.
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: Supporting comparator: indicates potential incremental value while limiting any expert-authorship claim.

claim: Expert-rubric scores had lower reported judge variance.
decision_use: Supporting: suggests improved repeatability, but only as a pilot measurement property.

claim: Generic-rubric scores had higher reported judge variance.
decision_use: Supporting comparator: strengthens the repeatability rationale but not a validity or readiness claim.

claim: Automated individual output grades weakly matched human judgments.
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: Disconfirming: requires human review and blocks autonomous adoption.

claim: Automated individual skill grades weakly matched human judgments.
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: Disconfirming: requires independent process annotation and human oversight.

claim: The configured-system skill ablation was small and heterogeneous.
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: Disconfirming: supports only a controlled pilot, not a stable causal or general adoption claim.

claim: One configured system had a large reported directional gain.
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: Supporting but narrow: motivates replication rather than immediate adoption.

claim: Some no-skill runs deployed, so deployment is not uniquely attributable to the skill.
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: Disconfirming: requires paired controls that separate content, integration, and randomness.

claim: Pairwise winners did not uniformly meet the human readiness boundary.
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: Disconfirming: requires absolute readiness checks by multiple experts rather than relying on relative preference.
